programme.title: Sister, Sister: Ladies' Choice
programme.desc: Tia defies Lisa's wishes and interviews for a job

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Young at Heart
programme.desc: Tia and Tamera go clubbin' to catch the show of one of their favorite singers

programme.title: Sister, Sister: I Had a Dream
programme.desc: A vivid dream Tamera encounters famous African-Americans who encourage her to pursue her dreams

programme.title: Sister, Sister: You Had to be There
programme.desc: Roger shares Tia and Tamera's most embarrassing moments with their boyfriends

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Prom Night
programme.desc: Lisa surprises the twins with homemade dresses for their senior prom

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Shoeless
programme.desc: With the help of her grandfather's horse-drawn carriage and a pair of borrowed shoes from Tia, Tamera stars in a '90s-style Cinderella story.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Graduation
programme.desc: The twins are graduating from high school, but the future doesn't look as bright for Tia's boyfriend Tyreke, who's having a tough time getting his high school equivalency degree.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Home Sweet Dorm
programme.desc: Tia and Tamera are off to college but they encounter housing problems during their search for the perfect dorm situation; Ray looks forward to remodeling their room

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Stop In The Name Of Fun
programme.desc: All work and no play makes Tia a dull girl... until Tamera signs her up for a spot on an MTV talent show!
programme.sub-title: Stop In The Name Of Fun

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Home Court Advantage
programme.desc: At Tamera's urging, Lisa hires a new assistant, Todd Banks, an attractive young male graduate who turns out to be lazy on the job. After a number of screw-ups, Lisa fires him.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: We Are Family
programme.desc: Ray's run for State Senate has his family up in arms after their lives become an open book in the press.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: The Grass Is Always Finer
programme.desc: Tia's crush on a handsome teacher leaves her shaken, stirred, and on the rocks with her boyfriend.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Bum Rap
programme.desc: Tia's experiment has Jordan and Tyreke at each other's throats, while Tamera and her dad face off in a political showdown!

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Domino Effect
programme.desc: Jordan and Tyreke find themselves escorting two young coeds to the Homecoming Dance --- until girlfriends Tia and Tamera decide to show up at the last minute!

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Greek To Me
programme.desc: Tia faces a tough choice among competing sororities; while Jordan also learns a lesson about the Greek.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: My Father's House
programme.desc: Jordan confronts his fears when Tamera and friends convince him that love is a two-way street.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: For The People
programme.desc: Ray is frustrated that his political campaign is not allowing him the time to get to the heart of the issues with his constituents. His campaign manager has been driving Ray hard, and the results are starting to make an impact.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Twins Or Consequences
programme.desc: Tia gets cold feet after agreeing to take an exam for her twin sister.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Mixed Doubles
programme.desc: It's a game of musical dates as the girls' family and friends switch partners for a night on the town.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Two Guys, A Girl, and a Calendar
programme.desc: Tia and Tamera battle it out on behalf of their guys as competition gets tight for a spot in their school's 12 Most Gorgeous Men on Campus calendar.
programme.sub-title: Two Guys, A Girl, and a Calendar

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Sweet Talk
programme.desc: Tamera lands a job as a radio talk show hostess dispensing advice on love on romance, including her own sister Tia's -- but without Tia's permission!

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Father's Day
programme.desc: The twins think a photojournalist visiting campus may be their biological father.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: I Know What You Did In Drama Class
programme.desc: Diavian and Tyreke become partners in drama class but Tia flips out after catching them in a passionate kiss during a drama rehearsal. Tamera and Jordan try to hook up cable in the girl's room
programme.sub-title: I Know What You Did In Drama Class

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Double Talk
programme.desc: Jordan's guest shot on Tamera's radio show turns into a full-time gig, but it may cost them their friendship.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Freaknik
programme.desc: Tia, Tamera, and Diavian all go to FreakNik in Atlanta during Spring Break. Tyreke races to Atlanta hoping to make up with Tia, but it may not be in time to save their relationship.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Before There Was Hip-Hop
programme.desc: An old-time jazz legend fuels dreams of the swing era for the class and dreams of heroics for Jordan.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Let Them Eat Cupcake
programme.desc: A series of mix-ups delays Victor's marriage proposal to Lisa

programme.title: Sister, Sister: The Road Less Traveled
programme.desc: Tia considers taking a summer internship with the WNBA in New York. Meanwhile, Lisa worries about her upcoming wedding.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Fly Away Home
programme.desc: Lisa gets cold feet on her wedding day, while Tia and Tamera entertain an anxious crowd waiting for the ceremony.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: The Meeting
programme.desc: Once separated at birth, identical twins Tia and Tamera, rediscover each other and scheme to live under one roof with their adoptive parents, who have nothing in common but their love for the girls.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: First Dates
programme.desc: Tia and Tamera vie for dates to the big school dance, while Ray and Lisa clash over the rules of the house.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Slumber Party
programme.desc: The boyfriend of one of Tia and Tamera's slumber party guests crashes the bash, while Lisa discovers that her beau is stepping out with another woman.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Cheater Cheater
programme.desc: Tamera cajoles Tia into helping her cheat on a test in order to win Ray's approval.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Wedding Bells & Box Boys
programme.desc: Lisa blames Ray for leading a bridal client astray, while Tia and Tamera see Roger in a whole new light during a double date.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Out Alone
programme.desc: While taking a bus to the movies, Tia and Tamera fight over the disadvantages of being a twin, but when they get lost, they must team up to find their way home.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: The Pimple
programme.desc: When a pimple pops up just before Tia's big date, Tamera agrees to step in for her -- and finds herself falling for Tia's guy.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Car Trouble
programme.desc: Tamera scratches the fender of her father's prized car, then tries to hide the truth with the help of Tia and Roger.

programme.title: Flashpoint
programme.desc: Team One struggles with a secret that could compromise their drug bust and end Wordy's career.

programme.title: Flashpoint
programme.desc: When Raf, a new recruit, joins Team One, he learns what it means to be a member of the Strategic Response Unit on one of their toughest days of the year forhostage and suicide situations.

programme.title: Flashpoint
programme.desc: Two bombs are set off while Sam, Spike and Raff are responding to a Hot Call at an office building, trapping them and nine civilians inside with a third seemingly non-diffusible bomb.

programme.title: Flashpoint
programme.desc: Team One has an unusual case when a passenger jet is hijacked by a group demanding the release of a prisoner.

programme.title: Flashpoint
programme.desc: Team One responds to 911 call where a masked gunman threatens a group of teens and kidnaps one of them.

programme.title: Flashpoint
programme.desc: Team One thinks they have a simple case of robbery on their hands,but things get complicated when the victim sabotages the rescue.

programme.title: Flashpoint
programme.desc: Team One is alerted to a situation in a bank where a woman who appears under duress is withdrawing all her money. When she sabotages her own rescue and escapes with her tormentor, the team turn their attention to her family, who is also in danger.

programme.title: Flashpoint
programme.desc: As Donna gets ready to walk down the aisle, things changed drastically. She learns some surprising news regarding a mentor she once worked with.

programme.title: Flashpoint
programme.desc: Team One must figure out how to get a Chinatown gang to release a local shop owner, then they also have to figure out how to get the one who shot the owner's daughter free as well.

programme.title: Flashpoint
programme.desc: An ex-cop with a grudge against Greg Parker takes him hostage and threatens to drop him off an arena's megatron if Greg can't talk him down. Meanwhile his team discovers that they are helpless to use tactical options to save their boss.

programme.title: Flashpoint
programme.desc: When Spike answers Sam's sister's desperate plea for help, he is forced, at gunpoint, to help two criminals steal evidence from the fortress that is the Metro Evidence Depot.

programme.title: Flashpoint
programme.desc: Team One is called in to try and stop a conflict at a psychiatric hospital where a stabbing has just taken place.

programme.title: Flashpoint
programme.desc: Greg Parker immediately faces suspension from the team after keeping the relationship between two team members a secret.

programme.title: Flashpoint
programme.desc: A fire captain who feels nothing he does is good enough is given the chance to prove to himself and to an injured member of his crew that he can set things right.

programme.title: Flashpoint
programme.desc: Team One is looking for an armed and abusive man who has tracked down his ex-wife demanding to know where their daughter, who is celebrating her 18th birthday, is.

programme.title: Flashpoint
programme.desc: Team One has to rescue a doctor, but as they search Sam discovers a child in critical need of medical attention, and realizes that being taken hostage may be the only way to save him.

programme.title: Flashpoint
programme.desc: Team One believes they are stopping a robbery in progress unaware that part of the robbers' plan is to escape their cruel leader. Their plan could make them his next victims and the team has to try to keep the robbers from harm.

programme.title: Flashpoint
programme.desc: Team One discovers that they need to take new security measures to protect the system they use to follow crimes after they are given acces to a crime about to happen.

programme.title: Flashpoint
programme.desc: Team One's efforts to save a kidnapped woman's life are dependent in large part on the abductor's brother, who holds a dark family secret.

programme.title: Flashpoint
programme.desc: Team One confronts a man desperate to keep the promise he made to his deaf nephew by protecting the only place the boy has ever felt safe.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Today in Teletubbyland, the Teletubbies are being quiet. A teddybear does a dance for the Teletubbies and the Teletubbies watch a little girl tap dancing. The Teletubbies go walking all around Teletubbyland.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies watch some children making marks. They use paint and different objects to make a colourful picture.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies watch a little girl make some Bagels with her uncle. It’s time for Tubby Custard. Laa-Laa sits down to eat her Tubby Custard, but she waits for Po.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Today, the Teletubbies are relaxing, until it is time to watch Andy Brown and some children look in a stream to see what they can find. The Teletubbies are trying to be quiet, but Po is making too much noise.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Tubbies are excited to see seven clouds appear in Teletubbyland. Some children learn about the number seven and in Teletubbyland, a voice trumpet rises and teaches the Tubbies to count to seven.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies play a hello and goodbye game in the hills. They watch a little boy look for a chameleon in the garden

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies watch some children playing with torches. In Teletubbyland, Po chases Tinky Winky, and Po catches him.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies watch three boys fill a sand pendulum and make a picture. Tinky Winky, Dipsy and Laa-Laa follow Po everywhere. The animals march through Teletubbyland, before it is time for Tubby Bye-Bye.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies watch some children play with balls of different shapes and sizes. In Teletubbyland, something happens to Dipsy's hat.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies love playing "Boo!" The Teletubbies watch some children learn about the number six. Tinky Winky, Dipsy and Laa-Laa get their knees dirty and have fun getting them clean.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Dipsy has no time for Tubby Custard, before watching a little girl help feed her baby sister. Po makes some Tubby Custard for Dipsy, but every time she comes to the table, she forgets and eats it all herself.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: All four Teletubbies stride across Teletubbyland to say hello. The Teletubbies watch a little girl take her rabbit to the vet.
programme.sub-title: I Want to be a Vet

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies watch a little girl visit her cousin to stay the night. The Teletubbies are very busy with their favourite things.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: A voice trumpet rises and makes the Teletubbies laugh. They watch some children exploring the colour green. The Teletubbies love green.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Andy Brown and some children go for a walk to find some red squirrels. The Teletubbies are playing a hiding game. Where are they? Can Noo-Noo find them?

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Five voice trumpets rises and plays some lovely music for the Teletubbies, who dance around Home Hill. Two girls have brought some new Tropical fish.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies appear one at a time to say ‘Eh-oh’. They watch some children playing with bubbles. It’s time for Tubby Custard. The custard machine makes a funny noise, and a tubby custard bubble comes out followed by more.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Po wants some Tubby Custard, but she doesn' t have time to eat is before watching some children do some painting in the garden. The Tubbies are very careful not to spill their Tubby Custard, but if they do, the Noo Noo will tidy up.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies watch some children go on a boat trip to see some seals. Tinky Winky is wondering what everyone is doing.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Tinky Winky is looking for the other Teletubbies. He can’t see any of them so he shouts out ‘Big Hug’ and all the Teletubbies come running for a big hug.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies watch a little boy called Brennan pretend to fly to the moon. In Teletubbyland, the Teletubbies are going out to play.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies love to play slide games. They stop when it is time to watch some children go on a train journey. Po loves to ride her scooter, but one day it rolls away and Po chases it all over Teletubbyland.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Po invites Tinky Winky to dance, and they dance all around Home Hill. Tamzin Griffin pretends to be Funny Lady and tells the story of the very proud crown.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: In Teletubbyland, some voice trumpets rise and make some funny animal noises. They baa like sheep, moo like cows, meow like cats and oink like pigs.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies join in with the game when a voice trumpet sings the Grand Old Duke of York. The magic windmill brings a musical transmission featuring King Pleasure and The Biscuit Boys.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies watch some children crawling. In Teletubbyland, the Teletubbies are crawling. Tinky Winky crawled along the path, Dipsy crawled over the hill, Laa-Laa crawled between the trees and Po crawled around the flowers.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies watch a girl a called Rebecca, who shows us her dogs doing some tricks. A swing appears in Teletubbyland, and Laa-Laa plays on the swing.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies dance a walking dance. They stop to watch a group of children are riding on a rickshaw. In Teletubbyland, Po is riding her scooter.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Some children learn about the number four, and the Teletubbies decide to go into the house. One by one, they leave. So, who is in the house?

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Tinky Winky & Po burst out of the house to say Eh-oh and enjoy a big hug. Then they do it again. They join Dipsy & Laa-Laa to watch a little girl read the story of Jack & The Beanstalk.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: A voice trumpet rises to play some double bass music for Laa-Laa. The Tubbies watch some children visit their friend who plays the double bass.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: A voice trumpet appears and teaches the Teletubbies a winter nursery rhyme. Po’s present is a little frosty cloud that begins to snow.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Tubbies play a bumping bottoms game. They stop to watch some children play some ball games. Laa-Laa’s ball goes in a puddle, she tries to get it back but can’t reach it.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies watch some children making Christmas cards. Laa-Laa’s present is some lovely decorations. When Noo-Noo tidies them up they tickle his throat, so he throws them out to decorate Home Hill.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Dipsy is busy at the switch panel, when he remembers the snow. The Tubbies watch an international Christmas insert. The Teletubbies are walking very carefully because snow can be very slippery.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies watch some children go for a ride on a Sea Tractor. Po goes for a ride on her scooter, while she’s riding she sees Laa-Laa’s ball, Dipsy’s hat and Tinky Winky’s bag. They’ve left them all behind!

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: Robert’s come to the valley to spend some quality time with Bob. They have a plan to spend the afternoon birdwatching – Robert wants to see the very rare lesser-striped sunflower warbler.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: Bob’s job today is to repair an eroded riverbank. There will be LOTS of mud to shift and Muck is beside himself with excitement. Before Muck can get stuck in, Bob needs him to fetch some equipment from JJ’s.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: Bob is building a new swimming pool for the Mayor. He asks Muck to collect some special tiles from JJ, explaining that they are very fragile and easy to break.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: Bob is building a pavilion in the park to celebrate the annual flower show. Molly has built a special statue of a horse for the occasion which is to be unveiled at the opening ceremony.

programme.title: Trains and Treehouses
programme.desc: Bob and his ‘Can-Do’ crew are back on site with a brand new adventure! Join Bob as he builds a train set for Mr Bentley as a birthday surprise and helps find a family of squirrels a new tree-house home.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: Whilst Spud is collecting some eggs for breakfast, he spots the large amount of straw delivered the previous day and overhears Bob and Farmer Pickles having a conversation. They discuss how they are making “something new out of straw”.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: Mr Bentley is organising a winter fair in the town hall square and enlists Bob to help set up the stalls, lights and the centrepiece of the fair – a giant inflatable Santa Claus.

programme.title: The Legend of the Golden Hammer
programme.desc: Our first sight of Fixham is in the episodic special The Legend Of The Golden Hammer. The Legend of the Golden Hammer consists of six stories in which Bob and his team tackle building jobs at six of Fixham’s key locations.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: The Mayor decides that the town needs a drive-in cinema and Bob is asked to build it out in the countryside. The first film to be shown will be “The Invasion of the Giant Bug.”

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: Today Bob and the team have a very exciting job to do. They are building a bridge over the river to where Mr Sabatini’s new bakery will be. Bob promises Scrambler that he can be the first machine across the bridge.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: Bob and the team are finishing refurbishing the old watermill ready to begin milling the first Sunflower Valley flour. Work is just about to start when Dizzy hears a squeaking noise coming from above. There are bats in the old mill!

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: Bob and Wendy are converting an old shop into a brand new restaurant for Mr Sabatini. Mr Sabatini asks Wendy to be the guest of honour at the grand opening. She is delighted but terrified at the prospect of giving a speech in public.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: Farmer Pickles is having problems with mice eating his barley seed so he asks Bob to build a seed store to keep it safe. Whilst Bob is working, Farmer Pickles decides to go and buy some replacement seed.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: Farmer Pickles wants a fence built around his compost heap, as Scruffty keeps getting into it. Muck is excited at the prospect of such a mucky job. JJ sends Trix up to the farm with the materials.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: Bob and the team are building a special flat-packed eco-home for Marjorie. When Muck and Benny spot Marjorie’s canvas tent and Spud’s straw-house, they wonder what else houses can be made from.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: Bob and the gang leave to convert a house into two separate flats. Mr Bentley, meanwhile, has been left in charge of The Mayor’s dog.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: Bob is building a new exercise park which Mr Dixon’s brother David, a famous goalkeeper, is going to open. Dizzy tries to cajole Spud into joining in a game of soccer, but uncharacteristically the scarecrow doesn’t want to play.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: Bob has a day off and he takes a trip to Farmer Pickles farm to take some photographs for the town photography competition. Wendy, meanwhile, has been called to the school hall where a pipe has burst, threatening the competition.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: Bob is erecting a tent to accommodate some newcomers to the valley. Mr Bentley is organising everything, but unfortunately leaves his phone with Spud who promptly declares himself ‘Mr Bentley’s Assistant’.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: It’s Mr and Mrs Bentley’s twenty fifth wedding anniversary and to celebrate Mr Bentley asks Bob and the gang to build a special summer house. Mrs Bentley is out for the day and the summer house is to be a surprise.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: Today’s the day that the machines get to build their brand new shelter. It’s going to be built into the side of a hill and made using found materials. Lofty, who slept outside at the old yard, decides that he’d like a place in the new shelter.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: Bob has been asked to install a new air conditioning unit at Mr Sabatini’s restaurant. Wendy and Molly meanwhile have been asked to redecorate the library at the school.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: It’s a big day. The team are building Farmer Pickles new factory which will turn sunflowers into sunflower oil. Everyone is excited to meet Marjorie, who arrives by bicycle. Marjorie has been brought on board by Farmer Pickles to manage the factory.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: An early morning start for the sunflower valley team, as they are moving logs in preparation for a safety fence around Mr Sabatini’s windmill. Wendy’s off to the sunflower oil factory to help Marjorie with a big delivery.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: Farmer Pickles is about to declare his Sunflower Oil business open, but there’s just one more job that he needs Bob to do. He needs a depot to store all the bottles of oil.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: Scoop and Dizzy have spent the night at Bob’s old yard in Bobsville to help Wendy with some digging and mixing. The machines all wish that Wendy could come and live out in Sunflower Valley.

programme.title: Green Builds and Recycling Centres
programme.desc: Bob’s back On Site! Join Bob as he shows you the amazing world of eco homes and recycling centres and learn how things you throw away can be used to make something new.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: Bob is building a marshland walkway with his Dad’s help. Benny hears that the marsh is ‘like a jungle’ and becomes determined to finds some jungle animals like tigers. Every time he spots one, however, it turns out to be something very ordinary.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: Bob is repairing a leaking water tank at the school, when he discovers bats in the belfry. He tapes up the tank temporarily, but says the bats will need a new home as they are a protected species.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: A fierce storm is forecast for Bobsville. At the farm Travis stays safe and snug in his shed, while Spud takes cover in a large barn. The storm causes a lot of damage and a tree falls on Spud’s barn – he’s okay, but his nose is a bit squashed!

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: An observation tower is going to be added next to the Visitor’s centre to provide a scenic view of Sunflower Valley. The scaffolding is raised and Roley prepares a path to connect the tower to the Dome.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: The team are all ready to set up Mr Sabatini’s bakery opposite the windmill so it’s close to the flour. Bob promises a surprise when they get there, and who should roll out from behind a bush but Benny!

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: Farmer Pickles asks Bob to build him a roadside stall where he can sell ‘Pick Your Own’ produce. He has even had a Giant Carrot sign made to go on top of the stall to advertise his wares.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: It’s a blistering hot day on Sunflower Valley. Bob and Wendy have got to finish building their workshop – making the workbenches and installing the power tools – it’s an inside job so the machines all get the day off.

programme.title: Race to the Finish
programme.desc: Mayor Bentley announces that Sunflower Valley is to stage its very first athletics tournament. A team from Sunflower Valley is to compete against a team of visiting athletes. Plus there is to be a special machine event!

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: Bob has bought a new attachment for Benny to try. It looks a bit big and heavy, but Benny is desperate to try it out so Bob promises he can as long as he’s with him to help. Bob gets an excited phone call from Sandy Beech up at the cave house.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: Bob is rendering a wall for Mr Sabatini. The job is straightforward, but the finished result looks rather plain, so Bob offers to get some tiles from JJ’s yard to decorate it. While Bob is gone, Mr Sabatini demonstrates his soccer skills to Dizzy.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: Bob is building a Seaweed Farm for Annie Pickles – Farmer Pickles cousin. Scrambler volunteers to help Annie collect seaweed seedling on the beach.

programme.title: Homes and Playgrounds
programme.desc: Join Bob and his “Can-Do” crew as they go back on site to show you the exciting real world of big machines! From bird’s nests to houses, we all need a home and in this brand new adventure Bob explains how they are built.

programme.title: Mega Machines: The Movie
programme.desc: Scoop, Muck and Lofty can’t wait to help Bob with his biggest build ever – building a dam and transforming an old quarry into a reservoir to provide Spring City with fresh water.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: Mr. Beasley decides to grow pineapples, and the best way top do this is to build a pineapple pit – a special pit, heated by horse manure! Travis wants to help and decides to fetch some horse manure from Farmer Pickles farm in Bobsville as quick as he can.

programme.title: Knights of Can-A-Lot
programme.desc: Bob, Wendy and the team get a big surprise when Bob's father Robert comes to visit. When stern Ms Chalmers from the National Heritage asks Bob to renovate the old castle a case of mistaken identity leaves Bob’s father in charge!

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: Bob’s too busy to even tell the machines what the job is for today. He takes Scrambler to the dome and the others follow. It turns out that they are going to turn the whole of the Dome into a Visitors centre and its opening tomorrow!

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: Mr Ellis has a batch of Doves delivered, to use in his magic act, and asks Bob to build a dovecote for his new birds to live in. Mr Ellis realises that he has to go to work but does not want to leave the birds at home alone.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: The Mayor asks Bob to build a Pets Corner in the local park. Farmer Pickles has picked up a selection of animals for it – including a chinchilla – and sends Travis and Spud off to deliver them to Bob. Unfortunately Bob runs out of chicken wire.

programme.title: Bob's Big Plan
programme.desc: When Bob and the team are building an extension to the offices of Mr Adams the local architect, Bob hears of a competition to build a brand new town in nearby Sunflower Valley, a place Bob knows very well from his childhood. Mr Adams shows Bob his idea.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: Bob and the gang are replacing some old pipes outside the town hall. Molly, meanwhile, is practicing for her first aid exam with a reluctant JJ as guinea pig. When Wendy calls JJ’s yard to order a special pipe and Molly offers to deliver it.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: Mr and Mrs Sabatini have asked Bob to convert a local house into a delicatessen. Unfortunately for Roley, there is no rolling to be done. The gang leaves to get on with the job and Roley decides to tag along just in case.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: Jeremy Bamber
programme.desc: An investigation into Jeremy Bamber's brutal murder of his family in Essex, which occurred in 1985. Sentenced for life, he still maintains he is innocent. Is the right person behind bars?

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: Garry Newlove
programme.desc: Garry Newlove’s wife Helen talks about the events on the night of his murder and the days afterwards, as well as her current work as a peer in the House of Lords combatting anti-social behaviour.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: Peter Tobin
programme.desc: Peter Tobin is one of the most notorious serial killers in the UK. From 1991–2006 he raped and murdered three young girls; Dinah McNicol, Vicky Hamilton and Angelika Kluk. Yet even prior to these horrific murders he had a history of violence.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: Stephen Port
programme.desc: A chilling look at the crimes of Stephen Port, the east London serial killer who poisoned four young men with lethal doses of a date rape drug.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: Manchester
programme.desc: The series returns to take a closer look at the most horrific crimes ever committed on British soil, starting with the May 2017 Manchester Arena bombing.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: Colin Ireland
programme.desc: In the summer of 1993, gay men in London were being terrorised by a brutal killer. Colin Ireland, a loner from Southend, had made a macabre resolution – to become a serial killer.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: Stephen Lawrence
programme.desc: On the 20th anniversary of his death, the family of Stephen Lawrence reflect on his tragic murder in 1993, as well as their lengthy fight for justice.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: Jimmy Savile
programme.desc: Jimmy Savile: From national treasure to one of the UK's most notorious sex offenders: this is the story of how the revelation of Jimmy Savile's crimes left the UK reeling.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: The Philpotts
programme.desc: When six children died in a house fire in Allenton, Derby in May 2012 it shocked the nation. Parents Mick and Mairead Philpott were charged with the murders. We uncover the reasons behind the arson attack on their own home.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: The Murder Of Jill Dando
programme.desc: Documentary examining the unsolved murder of British TV presenter and newsreader Jill Dando, who was shot outside her house in Fulham, London, in 1999.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: The Dunblane Massacre
programme.desc: In an emotional tribute, witnesses and victims' relatives look back on the tragic murder of 16 children and a teacher at Dunblane Primary School in Scotland, 15 years ago.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: April Jones
programme.desc: The hunt for five year old April Jones was the largest missing persons search in UK police history when she vanished in October 2012. But on October 6th a local man, Mark Bridger, was charged with her disappearance.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: Lee Rigby
programme.desc: In broad daylight on a busy Woolwich street on 22nd May 2013, serving soldier Fusilier Lee Rigby was attacked and murdered by Islamic extremists Michael Adebolajo and Michael Adebowale.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: April Jones
programme.desc: The hunt for five year old April Jones was the largest missing persons search in UK police history when she vanished in October 2012. But on October 6th a local man, Mark Bridger, was charged with her disappearance.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: Beverly Allitt
programme.desc: Beverley Allitt was thought to be a 'model' nurse. Very astute, caring and always willing to help, why did she murder four children and attempt to kill 11 others?

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: Suzy Lamplugh
programme.desc: A look at the case of estate agent Suzy Lamplugh, who showed a client around a house in Fulham in July 1986 and was never seen again. Dermot Murnaghan hosts.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: Steven Wright
programme.desc: A closer look at the crimes of Steve Wright, who was dubbed 'The Suffolk Strangler' after murdering five women who worked as prostitutes in Ipswich in 2006.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: John Sweeney
programme.desc: John Sweeney brutally murdered two of his girlfriends, dismembered their bodies and dumped them in canals. In a macabre twist, he hid clues in his abstract paintings, and five other girlfriends of his are currently still missing.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: Stephanie Slater
programme.desc: Estate agent Stephanie Slater reveals the story of her abduction, rape and incarceration, which occurred in 1992 whilst showing a man around a house.

programme.title: Girlfriends: Latching On And Lashing Out
programme.desc: Darnell and Maya’s re-marriage is off to a bumpy start.

programme.title: Girlfriends: Judging Edward
programme.desc: William confronts his emotionally distant father.

programme.title: Girlfriends: Everything Old Is New Again
programme.desc: An older man puts the moves on Joan.

programme.title: Girlfriends: Trial And Errors
programme.desc: Todd sues Toni for custody of Morgan.

programme.title: Girlfriends: Hot Girl On Girl Action
programme.desc: Jennifer’s keeping a surprising secret from Lynn

programme.title: Girlfriends: Sleeping Dogs
programme.desc: Maya accuses Darnell of infidelity.

programme.title: Girlfriends: My Business, Not Your Business
programme.desc: Joan fights for her restaurant’s survival.

programme.title: Girlfriends: All God's Children
programme.desc: Todd wants Morgan to convert to Judaism

programme.title: Girlfriends: The Music In Me
programme.desc: Lynn struggles to find her purpose in life.

programme.title: Girlfriends: The It Girl
programme.desc: Joan’s sex life is as hot as her restaurant.

programme.title: Girlfriends: Work In Progress
programme.desc: Darnell decides to pursue his dream career.

programme.title: Girlfriends: Oh Hell Yes: The Seminar
programme.desc: Maya excludes Lynn from her seminar on successful black women.

programme.title: Girlfriends: Game Over
programme.desc: Joan’s good fortune brings out the worst in Toni.

programme.title: Girlfriends: I'll Be There For You...But Not Right Now
programme.desc: Toni reunites with an old flame.
programme.sub-title: I'll Be There For You...But Not Right Now

programme.title: Girlfriends: The Game
programme.desc: NFL families cope with big-league pressures.

programme.title: Girlfriends: It's Raining Men
programme.desc: Joan dates three men simultaneously.

programme.title: Girlfriends: I Don't Want To Be A Player No More
programme.desc: Joan falls for an NBA player.
programme.sub-title: I Don't Want To Be A Player No More

programme.title: Girlfriends: Party Over Here...
programme.desc: Joan’s hard partying catches up with her.

programme.title: Girlfriends: ...Ain't Nothing Over There
programme.desc: Toni battles for custody of her daughter.

programme.title: Girlfriends: After the Storm
programme.desc: On Joan’s (Tracee Ellis Ross) last day in New Orleans volunteering helping the victims of Hurricane Katrina, she meets Aaron (Richard T. Jones), a fellow volunteer who asks her out.

programme.title: Girlfriends: In Too Deep
programme.desc: Joan (Tracee Ellis Ross) returns to work at the J-Spot only to find that William (Reggie Hayes) has put Monica (Keesha Sharp) in charge of managing the bar to get her out of his hair.

programme.title: Girlfriends: Bad Blood
programme.desc: Joan (Tracee Ellis Ross) starts to realize that Maya’s (Golden Brooks) house has become the hang-out spot and that she is no longer the center of the friendship.

programme.title: Girlfriends: Everybody Hate Monica
programme.desc: Joan (Tracee Ellis Ross) learns that Maya (Golden Brooks) and Lynn (Persia White) did not tell her about a recent outing they had with Toni, who was in town for a brief visit.

programme.title: Girlfriends: Hustle & Dough
programme.desc: In trying to seriously pursue her music career, Lynn (Persia White) informs the girls that she is performing at a local club during open mic night.

programme.title: Girlfriends: If You Can't Stand the Heat, Get Out of the Boonies
programme.desc: Maya (Golden Brooks) discovers Jabari (Kendre Berry) has been skipping school and plagiarized a major writing assignment.
programme.sub-title: If You Can't Stand the Heat, Get Out of the Boonies

programme.title: Girlfriends: Just Joan
programme.desc: When Monica and William apply for a marriage license, Monica’s shocked to learn he was previously married.

programme.title: Girlfriends: Karaoke-Dokee
programme.desc: Joan and Maya are appalled to find Lynn singing for money on the streets of L.A. She claims she’s got a paying gig at a local club, but that night, they’re aghast to find she’s the warm-up act at a karaoke bar.

programme.title: Girlfriends: He Had a Dream
programme.desc: Maya (Golden Brooks) begins to struggle with the responsibilities of homeownership and taking care of Jabari (Kendre Berry) while Darnell (Khalil Kain) is in Florida working for NASCAR.

programme.title: Girlfriends: I'll Have a Blue Line Christmas
programme.desc: Lynn (Persia White) is crushed when she receives a call from Big Boi (guest-starring as himself) saying that he is at a creative crossroads with the record company and he won’t be able to use her music.
programme.sub-title: I'll Have a Blue Line Christmas

programme.title: Girlfriends: Wrong Side of the Tracks
programme.desc: Maya (Golden Brooks) fears that Jabari (Kendre Berry) is growing up too fast after she and Darnell (Khalil Kain) witness an interaction between him and a young girl.

programme.title: Girlfriends: I Want My Baby Back
programme.desc: Maya (Golden Brooks) fears that Jabari (Kendre Berry) is growing up too fast after she and Darnell (Khalil Kain) witness an interaction between him and a young girl.

programme.title: Girlfriends: Hot for Preacher
programme.desc: When Lynn (Persia White) meets Eldon Perry (guest star Kadeem Hardison), an attractive guy at a club where she is singing, but she becomes conflicted about dating him when she discovers he is a Baptist minister.

programme.title: Girlfriends: Time to Man Up
programme.desc: During an evening out, Maya and Darnell befriend Alicia and Ray a new couple living in their neighborhood. When Maya witnesses an intense moment between the couple, she suspects that Ray is abusing Alicia.

programme.title: Girlfriends: Willie Or Won't He III: This Time It's Personal
programme.desc: After years of on-again, off-again dating and a pair of broken engagements, William and Monica finally tie the knot. At the reception, Robert Dooling, one of William’s biggest clients, thanks him for delaying his honeymoon to oversee an important merger.
programme.sub-title: Willie Or Won't He III: This Time It's Personal

programme.title: Girlfriends: What Had Happened Was...
programme.desc: After Aaron and Joan experience an uncomfortable moment when they run into one of Aaron's ex-girlfriends, they decide to discuss their past relationships with one another to avoid future awkward moments.

programme.title: Girlfriends: Church Lady
programme.desc: After splitting from Eldon over his objection to pre-marital sex, Lynn longs to get back together. She feels they share a special connection, but Eldon doesn’t appreciate Lynn’s attempts to seduce him.

programme.title: Girlfriends: A Dingo Ate My Dream House
programme.desc: Tired of all the inconveniences of living in the suburbs, Darnell (Khalil Kain) suggests to Maya (Golden Brooks) they should move back to the city, but Maya disagrees and does not want to give up her new home.
programme.sub-title: A Dingo Ate My Dream House

programme.title: Girlfriends: Operation Does She Yield
programme.desc: Ever since Monica left William, his life’s been a mess. He can’t work, eat or function normally. Because Joan, Maya, and Lynn feel responsible for driving Monica away, they decide they’ve got to do something to bring the couple back together.

programme.title: Girlfriends: A House Divided
programme.desc: When Lynn's (Persia White) new boyfriend Eldon (Kadeem Hardison), finds himself at odds with the church members over his choice of having Lynn as a girlfriend, he is faced with a tough decision.

programme.title: Girlfriends: To Be Determined / It's Been Determined
programme.desc: Joan (Tracee Ellis Ross) unexpectedly proposes to Aaron (guest star Richard T. Jones), but when he stumbles on his response, Joan decides the relationship is over and walks away.
programme.sub-title: To Be Determined / It's Been Determined

programme.title: 12 In A Box
programme.desc: 12 In A Box is the laugh-out-loud story of 12 former school friends who get together for a reunion they will never forget. Staring Miranda Hart.

programme.title: Timeline: Timeline
programme.desc: Based on the Michael Crichton novel. TIMELINE tells the story of a professor who inadvertently catapults himself back to 14th-century France, in the middle of the 100 Years War.

programme.title: Cold Souls: Cold Souls
programme.desc: Paul is an actor who feels bogged down by his participation in a production of Chekov's play, Vanya.

programme.title: The Cannonball Run
programme.desc: The classic comedy with cool stars and even cooler cars! Based on a true race event, Burt Reynolds leads an all-star cast in the ultimate quest to win The Cannonball Run.

programme.title: Evening
programme.desc: As Ann Lord reflects on one beautiful and life-changing weekend with the one true love of her life, her daughters come to their own understanding about the power of the past and the unbreakable bonds between mothers and daughters.

programme.title: Benji
programme.desc: Two school kids strike up a friendship with an orphaned puppy named Benji. When danger befalls them and they end up kidnapped by robbers who are in over their heads, Benji and his scruffy sidekick come to the rescue.

programme.title: Inside Out
programme.desc: After being released from prison after serving 13 years for manslaughter, AJ’s dreams of living a pleasant crime-free life soon come to an end when he discovers his closest childhood friend Jack, has been marked for death.

programme.title: Barely Lethal
programme.desc: While on a mission, Megan Walsh, an assassin, gets intrigued with the lives of the other teenagers who are her age. She decides to fake her own death and enroll herself in a school. Starring: Jessica Alba, Samuel L. Jackson, Sophie Turner, Jaime King

programme.title: A Good Woman
programme.desc: The seductive Mrs. Erlynne (Helen Hunt) travels to Amalfi, Italy, hoping to win over married aristocrat Robert Windermere. But when Windermere’s innocent wife Meg (Scarlett Johansson) hears of the scandal, she plots an elaborate revenge.

programme.title: World Sport
programme.desc: Highlights and action from the world's sporting competitions.

programme.title: Quest's World of Wonder
programme.desc: Richard Quest travels to a range of cities around the world. In the process, Richard discovers aspects of their rich culture, complex history and different ways of life that tourists do not know.

programme.title: CNN Newsroom Saturday
programme.desc: Updates of the latest news from around the world.

programme.title: CNN Newsroom Saturday
programme.desc: Updates of the latest news from around the world.

programme.desc: Bill Weir speaks with the biggest stars working to revolutionise the environmental impact of live music. Exploring their efforts to reform an industry known for its massive energy use and waste.
programme.sub-title: Change Amplified: Live Music and the Climate Crisis

programme.title: Connecting Africa

programme.title: Knight Rider
programme.desc: Michael and K.I.T.T. race against time to find the donor needed to save a young girl's life.

programme.title: Knight Rider
programme.desc: Michael and K.I.T.T. investigate the death of a motorcycle rider.

programme.title: Knight Rider
programme.desc: Part 2 of 2. Michael's old nemesis, Garth Knight (also played by Hasselhoff), kidnaps Devon, April, and a scientist to force a showdown between K.I.T.T. and his new and improved Goliath.

programme.title: Knight Rider
programme.desc: Michael is pursued by an international criminal.

programme.title: Knight Rider
programme.desc: Part 2 of 2. Michael Knight and K.I.T.T. are sent to investigate the murder of a Federal attorney.

programme.title: Knight Rider
programme.desc: Posing as a singer in a rock bank, Michael looks for a connection to the former lead singer's "accidental" death.

programme.title: Knight Rider
programme.desc: Michael and K.I.T.T. are buried alive at an excavation site.

programme.title: Knight Rider
programme.desc: Part 1 of 2. In the Season Three premiere, Michael and K.I.T.T. reunite with their old Foundation co-worker and friend, Bonnie Barstow.

programme.title: Knight Rider
programme.desc: Michael (David Hasselhoff) uses his trusty car, K.I.T.T. to embark on a search for $3 million in stolen diamonds.

programme.title: Knight Rider
programme.desc: When an old friend of Michael is seriously injured in a hit-and-run accident, he sets out to find the person responsible.

programme.title: Knight Rider

programme.title: Knight Rider
programme.desc: Michael and K.I.T.T. meet their match in a seemingly indestructible adversary -- K.A.R.R. -- K.I.T.T.'s alter ego.

programme.title: Knight Rider
programme.desc: Michael is framed and must vindicate himself while stopping a madman's sale of a biological warfare agent.

programme.title: Knight Rider
programme.desc: Michael (David Hasselhoff) tries to catch up with an evil group that is planning to sell poisonous substances to terrorists.

programme.title: Knight Rider
programme.desc: Two masked men (Anthony James, Karl Johnson) steal 1,000 pounds of highly explosive dynamite from a guarded trailer. With David Hasselhoff, Jason Bateman.

programme.title: Psych
programme.desc: After a woman tells the SBPD she witnessed a murder in broad daylight, Shawn and Gus are the only ones who believe her and therefore protect her by hiding her at a remote commune whose inhabitants label themselves as progressive intellectuals.

programme.title: Psych
programme.desc: Shawn and Juliet's romantic getaway vacation from casework and the SBPD is interrupted when their hotel room is robbed and a dead body surfaces at a nearby winery. Guest Stars: Tony Hale, Jason Priestley, Arden Myrin.

programme.title: Psych
programme.desc: Despereaux, Shawn and Gus race against an evil gang of art thieves to find a mythical dagger that, according to legend, will unlock the hidden treasure of a deceased billionaire. Cary Elwes guest stars.
programme.sub-title: Indiana Shawn And The Temple Of The Kinda Crappy, Rusty Old Dagger

programme.title: Psych
programme.desc: After Lassiter moves into a new condominium building and strange things start happening to him, he is forced to hire Shawn and Gus to figure out what could be causing the supposed paranormal disturbance.

programme.title: Psych
programme.desc: Shawn and Gus go undercover to investigate the attempted murder of a contestant on a show called "Paths of Love", a dating show where eligible men compete for the affections of a woman they have all crossed paths with in the past but never actually met.

programme.title: Psych
programme.desc: When the founder of "Right Now", Santa Barbara's oldest at-risk youth program, is shot and almost killed, Shawn, Gus and a few members of Gus's a capella group from college investigate to find the culprit.

programme.title: Scorpion: Satellite Of Love
programme.desc: The new Director of Homeland Security reunites the team when a nuclear powered Russian satellite is knocked out of orbit and must be diverted before it detonates over Southern California.

programme.title: Scorpion: Cuba Libre
programme.desc: Team Scorpion heads to Cuba when a woman from Cabe's past pleads for him to help catch a Serbian war criminal.

programme.title: Scorpion: Fish Filet
programme.desc: In order to save the lives of three judges, Sylvester goes undercover in federal prison but Team Scorpion must break him out when his life is threatened. Kevin Weisman guest stars.

programme.title: Skins: Maxxie and Anwar
programme.desc: Anwar and Maxxie's friendship is tested when they go on the most disastrous school trip ever.

programme.title: Skins: Michelle
programme.desc: When a shining knight appears on the horizon with the promise of a happy ending, Michelle jumps right in.

programme.title: Skins: Effy
programme.desc: When Effy goes missing, Tony is desperate to find her and hopes he can rely on his old mates for help.

programme.title: Skins: Finale
programme.desc: It’s Anwar’s birthday, but everyone is too caught up in their own personal problems to celebrate.

programme.title: Skins: Tony and Maxxie
programme.desc: Maxxie wants to leave college and follow his dream to become a dancer, but his dad would rather see him continue in the family business.

programme.title: Skins: Sketch
programme.desc: Maxxie has this creepy feeling someone is watching him.

programme.title: Mr. D: Gerry Gets Gym
programme.desc: Gerry is asked to temporarily fill in for an absent phys-ed teacher.

programme.title: Mr. D: Self Defense
programme.desc: Gerry 'helps' Bobbi with her girls’ self defense class.

programme.title: Mr. D: Graduation
programme.desc: Gerry has a positive interview for a position with Xavier’s main rival school.

programme.title: Mr. D: Mafia Dad
programme.desc: Gerry is finally given an opportunity to coach the varsity boys’ basketball team but finds himself in an awkward position with a player’s dad.

programme.title: Mr. D: Staff Hangover
programme.desc: Morning assembly reveals the Xavier staff in rough shape. Last night’s staff party went late and got messy.

programme.title: Mr. D: President Jimmy
programme.desc: Gerry becomes unexpectedly embroiled in the school’s student council election and is forced to seek the help of an old foe.

programme.title: Mr. D: Callaghan’s Daughter
programme.desc: Callaghan’s daughter is in town visiting, and after seeing her old graduation photo, Gerry wants take her out on a date.

programme.title: Mr. D: Dating Maya Student
programme.desc: Gerry’s former pupil, Maya is back at Xavier as a student teacher and Gerry is having difficulty accepting her as an adult.

programme.title: Mr. D: Short Stocked
programme.desc: Gerry and Bill’s house they rent is up for sale and Gerry uses the advice of a market-savvy student raise enough money for a down payment.

programme.title: Earth: Final Conflict: Defector
programme.desc: A shuttle crashes on a beach after being pursued by a second Taelon piloted shuttle. When Liam locates the survivor (a Taelon called Belleye), he claims to want to help the Resistance take back Earth from the Taelons.

programme.title: Earth: Final Conflict: Heroes and Heartbreak
programme.desc: Augur is recruited by a Taelon weapons project, but the others are worried about his involvement. When the project is launched earlier than originally planned to fight a Jiridian fleet, the Earth is in danger.

programme.title: Earth: Final Conflict: Message in a Bottle
programme.desc: A Jiridian probe headed for Earth is shot down by the Taelon Embassy and crashes outside of D.C. Liam recovers the probe, but he, Augur, and Julia are captured by another underground resistance movement led by the real Colonel Liam Kincaid.

programme.title: Earth: Final Conflict: Crossfire
programme.desc: As the election draws near, Liam believes Zo'or is trying to have Jonathan Doors murdered. The Resistance worries that an attack is imminent at the final debate. Julia mobilizes a group to protect Doors against Liam's wishes.

programme.title: Earth: Final Conflict: Crackdown
programme.desc: An unknown woman saves Augur and Liam from the raids on the Resistance. Lili sabotages the mother ship, but Zo'or stops its total destruction. Lili is reportedly dead. The investigation on the President's shooter continues.

programme.title: Earth: Final Conflict: The Vanished
programme.desc: Some who were detained during the Resistance sweep have not reappeared after their release. Lili is taken to a lab run by Sandoval and implanted. Renee tries to help Liam find the missing people.

programme.title: Earth: Final Conflict: Emancipation
programme.desc: A group posing as volunteers attacks using a stolen skrill prototype that does not require an implant to control. It is discovered that the skrill queen is also missing. The investigation leads to a resistance group called Black Wednesday.

programme.title: Earth: Final Conflict: Déjà Vu
programme.desc: A joint Doors International/Taelon venture creates a machine that allows people to vividly relive a memory, but Liam suspects a back alley version of the device is leading to the death of some users.

programme.title: Earth: Final Conflict: The Once and Future World
programme.desc: Artifacts believed to be Taelon are found in South America. Renee and Liam try to beat Sandoval to the source to determine the importance of the found pieces.

programme.title: Earth: Final Conflict: Thicker than Blood
programme.desc: An old business partner of Augur's is hired by Da'an to get information about the Resistance, resulting in the death of many members. Meanwhile, Sandoval finds out he has a life threatening blood disorder.

programme.title: Earth: Final Conflict: A Little Bit of Heaven
programme.desc: Liam and Renee investigate a dead pregnant woman missing her fetus. They discover a town that Da'an helped after a nuclear meltdown and a suspicious brother and sister who were part of an early experiment to combine human and Taelon DNA.

programme.title: Earth: Final Conflict: Pad'ar
programme.desc: Pad'ar is a fierce battle game where spectators can connect via a "jack patch" that Liam suspects is causing neurological damage to users. When a Pad'ar warrior dies, it raises even more questions about the safety.

programme.title: Earth: Final Conflict: In Memory
programme.desc: Lili wakes up, supposedly on Earth after a coma, and is told the Jaridians have come and conquered the Taelons. She remembers Sandoval telling her she no longer has and Earth-based physiology and believes things are not as they seem.

programme.title: Earth: Final Conflict: The Cloister
programme.desc: Zo'or and Da'an travel to a cloister for an energy sharing ceremony, but one of the sisters is killed and everyone is a suspect.

programme.title: Earth: Final Conflict: Interview
programme.desc: Zo'or agrees to do a live television interview to try to endear himself to humans, but the show's producer has an ulterior motive in getting him in front of a camera.

programme.title: Earth: Final Conflict: Keep Your Enemies Closer
programme.desc: The shuttle Liam and Zo'or are in crashes over wilderness, but survive in safety bubbles. The people they meet live off the earth and know nothing of the Taelons. However, Zo'or is injured and missing a Synod meeting on the mother ship.

programme.title: Earth: Final Conflict: Subterfuge
programme.desc: Sandoval has a mole attempting to gain information about Liam - particularly concerning his commitment to the Taelons. When a Taelon and protector are killed in a portal that malfunctions, Liam is suspicious that it was intentional.

programme.title: Earth: Final Conflict: Scorched Earth
programme.desc: Bombings begin at embassies and Liam believes they may reignite the SI (Sino-Indian) War, which ended with an armistice several years before. An old intelligence agency friend of Renee's has interesting holographic technology.

programme.title: Earth: Final Conflict: Sanctuary
programme.desc: Liam Kincaid races against the clock to find Zo'or, who disappears after contracting an ancient Taelon virus that has the potential to cause a devastating plague.

programme.title: Earth: Final Conflict: Through Your Eyes
programme.desc: Zo'or kidnaps a comatose policeman in order to use the man's body as a 'vessel' in a consciousness transfer experiment that will allow him to 'test' the human survival instinct and cause havoc on Earth.

programme.title: Earth: Final Conflict: Time Bomb
programme.desc: While examining Ma'el's ship, Liam and Renee find a chamber and in it are some life pods and the persons are all dead except for one.

programme.title: The Lost World: Prophecy
programme.desc: A fortune teller gives the explorers startling predictions that prove eerily accurate, with Malone receiving a grim fortune Meanwhile Challenger and Summerlee try and evade highly-evolved raptors.

programme.title: The Lost World: Barbarians at the Gate
programme.desc: The explorer's last hope of leaving the plateau is destroyed when Drakul and his men come after them and their hot air balloon. A war breaks out between Drakul and Tribune, now armed with gunpowder.

programme.title: The Lost World: All or Nothing
programme.desc: After watching Summerlee fall from a destroyed bridge, Marguerite and Veronica attempt to find him. Tribune rescues Roxton but expects payment in kind.

programme.title: The Lost World: Amazons
programme.desc: The men find themselves embraced by a tribe of Amazons and discover they are to be studs for the breeding ritual, but this purpose may come with a fatal end.

programme.title: The Lost World: Tourist Season
programme.desc: Challenger builds a windmill. But when his generator is struck by lightning, a portal is opened that ushers in a helicopter carrying a family of tourists from the year 2000.

programme.title: The Lost World: Stone Cold
programme.desc: An ominous castle draws three of the adventurers under its paranormal influence. Roxton, Marguerite, and Malone take on new personalities while the mansion entangles them in a love triangle.

programme.title: The Lost World: Divine Right
programme.desc: A riderless horse leads Roxton to a tribe that makes him king. He must either accept the title or face execution, while the rest of the group is captured.

programme.title: The Lost World: Skin Deep
programme.desc: When Marguerite begins acting uncharacteristically nice, the group raises some questions. A stumbling dinosaur in the jungle holds the answer: an alien life force has begun controlling their bodies.

programme.title: The Lost World: London Calling
programme.desc: Nelson discovers a map that shows a way off the plateau, but he has doubts about leaving. The group has to outrun nomads as they follow the route out of the Lost Word.

programme.title: The Lost World: The Prisoner
programme.desc: A shape-shifter convinces Veronica that her parents are in a place called El Dorado. When Veronica takes off without telling the others, she unwittingly releases a powerful giant from captivity.

programme.title: The Lost World: The Games
programme.desc: Empress Centuria orders her Lizardmen to capture Tribune. When her soldiers find him at the treehouse, he, Malone, Marguerite, and Roxton are imprisoned, and thrown into the Gladiators' Arena.

programme.title: The Lost World: The Source
programme.desc: With all of Lost World thirsting for water, the explorers are attacked by raptors. They are aided by a young woman, Ana, and Marguerite becomes interested in her knowledge of the Fountain of Youth.

programme.title: The Lost World: Trophies
programme.desc: A hunting party arrives on the plateau and takes fire at a Troglodyte child. Though they claim to be there to bring them home, Veronica becomes the target of their gunfire and snares.

programme.title: The Lost World: Voodoo Queen
programme.desc: An alluring stranger named Danielle seeks aide from Roxton and the others after her village is taken by Trogs. But when the woman uses a voodoo doll to bewitch Roxton, the group must rescue her.

programme.title: The Lost World: The Guardian
programme.desc: Challenger is trapped and taken to a village of orphans, protected by dense jungle and a plant that feeds on fresh meat. Marguerite and Veronica must help Challenger escape before they are killed.

programme.title: The Lost World: Under Pressure
programme.desc: A handsome stranger stumbles onto the plateau and becomes close with the explorers. While he gains the attention of Marguerite and Veronica, Challenger's electrostatic accumulator is targeted.

programme.title: The Lost World: The Outlaw
programme.desc: Roxton and Marguerite discover a way through the mountain and explore the fissure in hopes of finding a way off the plateau. Instead, they discover an English town and trouble with the locals.

programme.title: The Lost World: Quality of Mercy
programme.desc: Veronica and Malone are imprisoned at a labor camp occupied by emotionless prisoners, robotic wardens, and alien rulers called Prin. The explorers must plan a prison break to free their cohorts.

programme.title: The Lost World: Mark of the Beast
programme.desc: Marguerite and Roxton discover a strange amulet that leaves the mark of a dinosaur on their skin. Those with the imprint begin to tap into their more primitive instincts.

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Actors Laila Rouass and Charles Dale battle their way around the north of England for eccentric antiques they hope will turn a profit. James Lewis and Paul Laidlaw are there to help as they travel from York via Leeds to auction in Chertsey.

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: John Barrowman and Myleene Klass, with David Barby and David Harper, hunt for antiques to turn a profit. On the way, they visit Bristol, Chepstow, Newport and Cardiff before winding up at auction in Froncysylite by the Welsh-English border.

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: David Harper and David Barby help out former gold medalists Robin Cousins and Tessa Sanderson, ready to hit the road with £400. From Bury St Edmonds, with stops at the Whipple Museum and the Fan Museum, who will win the day in Greenwich?

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Fern Britton and Phil Vickery take a trip around Devon with Mark Stacey and James Braxton. The teams hunt treasures to make a profit in Crewkerne, Somerset, Fern learns more about Agatha Christie and Phil gets basic with bread making.

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: DJs Janice Long and Ken Bruce embark on a trip around Oxfordshire with Christina Trevanion and Paul Laidlaw. They're looking for antiques that will make money at auction, but spare a moment for buses and the early days of music recording.

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Dinnerladies Anne Reid and Thelma Barlow join Paul Laidlaw and David Harper for a trip around Oxfordshire. Armed with a classic car and £400 each, the teams take in wartime artwork then compete at auction in Penkridge, Staffordshire.

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Simon Williams and Duncan Preston join James Lewis and Philip Serrell for a trip around Essex, looking for items to auction in Torquay, Devon. On the way, Duncan tries an ancient craft and someone has a tale to tell about Boris Johnson.

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: The sixth Doctor Who, Colin Baker, joins the first Doctor's assistant, Peter Purves, exploring Herefordshire and Worcestershire in a TARDIS disguised as a 1970 Morris Minor. Experts Will Axon and James Braxton help them navigate.

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Tim Brooke-Taylor and Graeme Garden get back into character as they join Will Axon and Philip Serrell and take to the road in Devon. Along the way, these off-the-wall comedians enter the equally surreal world of two eccentric inventors.

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Hermione Norris and John Thomson join up with Mark Stacey and Catherine Southon on a classic car trip through Cornwall, aiming to buy antiques for auction in London. On the way, John learns nautical history and Hermione takes off.

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Lucy Alexander and Martin Roberts trade homes for antiques! Paired up with experts James Braxton and James Lewis, they scour Hampshire in search of bargains to sell at auction for profit--to benefit Children in Need.

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Frances Barber and Phil Davis join Catherine Southon and Thomas Plant on a buying spree around East Sussex. Armed with £400 and a classic car, they hear a gripping tale of eccentricity and attempted murder on the way to auction in London.

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Former MP and health minister Edwina Currie goes up against journalist Eve Pollard on a trip around Liverpool and Manchester. Paired with Paul Laidlaw and David Harper, Edwina goes underground and Eve sees a different side to woman's lib.

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: It's Weatherfield versus Walford, as Shaun Williamson takes on Vicky Entwistle. In a stunning TVR Chimera, with Paul Laidlaw and Mark Stacy, they're looking to see who can turn the biggest profit at auction.
